name: stackit
description: Inspect resources on STACKIT, the sovereign German cloud — list projects, servers, and machine types via the Resource Manager and IaaS APIs

envVars.name: STACKIT_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN
envVars.description: Access token for a STACKIT service account, sent as Authorization: Bearer. Obtained via the key flow (signed JWT exchanged at the service-account token endpoint) or the STACKIT CLI; tokens are short-lived and must be refreshed

tools.name: List Projects
tools.description: List STACKIT projects visible to the service account via the Resource Manager. At least one of containerParentId or member must be provided (use the service account's email as member).

containerParentId.description: Identifier of the parent resource container (organization or folder); containerId or UUID

member.description: Email address of the user or service account whose visible projects should be listed

limit.description: Maximum number of projects to return

offset.description: Offset of the first item to return

historicalSummary.omitted: labels, parent container details, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: List Servers
tools.description: List IaaS servers in a STACKIT project and region

historicalSummary.omitted: NICs, volume attachments, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Get Server
tools.description: Get a single IaaS server in a project and region, including status, machine type, and availability zone

tools.name: List Machine Types
tools.description: List available machine types (server flavors) in a project and region, useful before creating or resizing servers

filter.description: Filter expression over machine type fields, e.g. vcpus >= 4

historicalSummary.omitted: extra specs and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Start Server
tools.description: Boot up a stopped server, or allocate it again if it was deallocated (returns 202 Accepted; completes asynchronously)

tools.name: Stop Server
tools.description: Stop a server (returns 202 Accepted; completes asynchronously). Note: a stopped server remains on the hypervisor and is still charged full price for its attached resources

tools.name: Reboot Server
tools.description: Reboot a server (returns 202 Accepted; completes asynchronously); soft reboots ask the OS to shut down gracefully, hard reboots power-cycle

action.description: Reboot type: soft (graceful, default) or hard (power cycle)

prompts.title: Project overview
prompts.prompt: List my STACKIT projects, then list the servers in the project and region I name, with status and machine type.

prompts.title: Machine type sizing
prompts.prompt: Show me the STACKIT machine types available in my project and region and recommend one for a small web service.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: STACKIT setup

steps.title: Get a STACKIT account and project
steps.description: STACKIT is a B2B cloud by Schwarz Digital — request an organization at https://www.stackit.de, then create a project in the STACKIT Portal (https://portal.stackit.cloud).

steps.title: Create a service account with a key
steps.description: In the Portal, open your project > Service Accounts, create one, assign it a role (e.g. reader), and create a service account key (key flow). The legacy static token flow was removed on 17 December 2025.

steps.title: Obtain a short-lived access token
steps.description: Exchange a self-signed JWT (signed with the key's private RSA key) at the service account token endpoint, or simply run the STACKIT CLI: 'stackit auth activate-service-account --service-account-key-path key.json' and export the resulting access token.

steps.title: Set the environment variable
steps.description: Put the access token into your .env as STACKIT_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run List Projects with member set to the service account's email, then List Servers for a returned projectId in your region (e.g. eu01).

notes: Access tokens from the key flow are short-lived (refresh roughly every 10 minutes) — automate token renewal for long-running agents
List Projects requires at least one filter: containerParentId (organization/folder) or member (email)
The IaaS API v2 is the stable release; v1 is deprecated and will be removed after 1 March 2027
IaaS URLs are region-scoped via the {region} path segment, e.g. eu01
